data.title: USDCHF Technical Analysis – Some days of relief for the greenback
data.description:
Fundamental Overview

The US Dollar this week has been erasing some of the losses experienced last week after the benign US PPI and the Trump’s reciprocal tariffs announcement. There hasn’t been any notable catalyst this week to support the greenback although some analysts pointed to less dovish comments from Fed’s Waller on Tuesday which would explain the rise in Treasury yields afterwards.

On the CHF side, the Swiss CPI last week came in line with expectations although the core measure increased. It didn’t change much for the SNB, but the market scaled back the expectations for potential negative rates.

USDCHF Technical Analysis – Daily Timeframe

USDCHF Daily

On the daily chart, we can see that USDCHF bounced near the key 0.8960 support zone and pulled back into the broken trendline. The sellers will likely step in around these levels to position for a break below the support, while the buyers will want to see the price rising back above the trendline to start targeting the 0.92 handle next.

USDCHF Technical Analysis – 4 hour Timeframe

USDCHF 4 hour

On the 4 hour chart, we can see that we have a minor upward trendline defining the pullback into the major trendline. The buyers will likely lean on it to position for new highs, while the sellers will look for a break lower to increase the bearish bets into the 0.8960 support zone.

USDCHF Technical Analysis – 1 hour Timeframe

USDCHF 1 hour

On the 1 hour chart, we can see that the sellers stepped in around the most recent lower high to position for the pullback into the minor upward trendline. There’s not much else we can add here as the buyers will look for a bounce, while the sellers will look for a break. The red lines define the average daily range for today.

Upcoming Catalysts

Today we get the latest US Jobless Claims figures, while tomorrow we conclude the week with the US Flash PMIs.

data.title: Bosch to Shut Down Solid-Oxide Fuel Cells Business Amid Hydrogen Refocus
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Bosch said it would stop developing solid-oxide fuel-cell technology used to turn fuel into electricity, resulting in the possible loss of around 550 jobs, as part of a plan to focus on electrolysis hydrogen.

The company said Thursday that the decision comes as part of an operational and investment realignment of the business as the market hasn't developed as expected.

Bosch said it would focus on technologies for hydrogen production and the supply of electrolysis components, particularly proton-exchange membrane electrolysis stacks, which the company said is on track to debut in the segment this year.

"The conversion of hydrogen into electricity is not yet being given the necessary priority in Europe, and especially in Germany. This means that further engineering effort will be required in the years ahead, and this will significantly reduce commercial viability," the company said.

The solid-oxide fuel cells business employs around 550 workers, most of them in Germany, and Bosch will engage with representatives to decide about their future in the company.

In November, the company said it would cut up to 5,550 jobs across its operations in the coming years as the automotive sector looks to downsize in response to a tougher outlook. The group employed 417,900 people worldwide as of Dec. 31.

Bosch said Thursday that as part of the move, it will end its partnership with technology partner Ceres Power, with the Bosch representative on the Ceres Power supervisory board standing down with immediate effect.

Bosch will treat its minority interest in Ceres Power as a non-core financial investment, and might divest its stake in the future. It owns a 17.44% shareholding in the company.

By 2030, Bosch expects hydrogen technology to generate sales revenue running into billions of euros.

In a separate statement, Ceres Power noted Bosch's comment and said the decision won't have any impact on its current guidance.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Lowers Swedbank to Reduce as Rate Cuts, Weak Lending Weigh on Outlook
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe took a more cautious view on Swedbank's (SWED-A.ST) near-term prospects, cutting its 2025 and 2026 estimates while expecting a rebound in 2027.

The research firm lowered its rating to reduce from add, with a target price of 268 kronor.

For 2025, AlphaValue/Baader Europe now forecasts an EPS of 23.0 kronor, down from its prior estimate of 28.8 kronor. Analyst Sylvain Perret cited pressure from deeper rate cuts for the kronor and euro, weaker lending demand, and a "depressing" net interest income outlook as key factors behind the revision.

Swedbank's 2026 EPS estimate was also slashed by 16.6% to 24.9 kronor, as the analyst anticipates a gradual recovery in lending demand and a slow improvement in margins.

"Although the 2024 results came in above our projections, we project lower NII and fee income into 2025 and 2026, as we account for lower NIM, weaker lending demand, lower AM contribution and lower overall fee generation," the research firm wrote.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Slide as Fed Signals Rate Cuts on Hold; Lenovo Declines 6%
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks fell on Thursday, as indications that the US Federal Reserve was in no rush to slash interest rates weighed on investor sentiment.

The Hang Seng Index slid 1.6%, or 367.26 points, to end at 22,576.98. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index retreated 1.7%, or 140.54 points, to 8,322.56.

US Fed officials are inclined toward keeping interest rates steady as they wait for further progress on inflation and clarity surrounding the new administration's economic policies.

The interest rate is likely to be kept between 4.25%-4.5%, according to the minutes of the Fed's January meeting released in Washington on Wednesday.

Compounding the downbeat mood were jitters ahead of the earnings reports of Chinese tech giants Alibaba Group , Bilibili , and NetEase . Shares of the three firms closed lower on Thursday.

In corporate news, Lenovo Group declined over 6% despite doubling its attributable profit to $693 million for the three months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from $337 million a year prior.

data.title: China's iQIYI launches $300 million convertible bond, term sheet shows
data.description:
Chinese video streaming platform iQIYI has launched a $300 million convertible bond offering, showed a term sheet reviewed by Reuters on Thursday.

The unsecured senior notes, which have a maturity of five years, pay a coupon of 4.25% to 4.75% a year, payable quarterly, the term sheet showed.

iQiyi will use the proceeds for the repayment or repurchase of existing debt securities and for general corporate purposes.

Bank of America, Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan are joint bookrunners of the deal, the term sheet showed.

data.title: Airbus drops amid space business charges, production pressures
data.description:
** Shares in Airbus fall around 2% after it posted Q4 EBIT miss on Defense and Space (D&S) charges and warned of short-term production pressures

** Europe's largest aerospace group expects to deliver around 820 jets this year, below a consensus of 825 cited by Jefferies, and confirms delay to its A350 freighter by about a year to H2 2027

** Airbus took a fresh charge of 300 mln euros ($312.8 mln) for its troubled Space business, while highlighting potential risks to the long-term future of its slow-selling A400M military transport aircraft

** Jefferies points to a strong operating mix offset by charges on the D&S division, which dragged Q4 EBIT 2% below consensus

** J.P.Morgan says Q4 EBIT is "a touch light" and flags lower-than-expected 2025 delivery and EBITA targets

** The group forecasts adj. EBIT of around 7.0 bln euros for 2025, 3% below the current Bloomberg median consensus, JPM says

** The stock is among worst performers on France's CAC 40 index

data.title: Market Chatter: GM Korea Faces Possible Closure Amid Trump's Tariff Threats
data.description:
GM Korea, the South Korean unit of Detroit-based automaker General Motors heavily reliant on US exports, faces potential closure if US President Donald Trump imposes 25% auto tariffs, The Korea Herald reported Wednesday.

The automaker sold nearly 90% of its 474,735 exported units to the US last year, despite struggling in the domestic market, the report said.

The company could consider shutting down its Korean operations if tariffs make exports unviable, the report said, citing Daeduk University automotive engineering professor Lee Ho-geun.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: RailTel Corporation Secures INR224 Million Work Order
data.description:
RailTel Corporation of India has bagged a work order worth 224.4 million Indian rupees from the High Court of Jammu and Kashmir, and Ladakh, India, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company did not specify the nature of the services to be provided under the contract that is scheduled to be executed by Aug. 19.

Shares of the company were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Gerdau Q4 Earnings Decrease, Net Sales Increase
data.description:
Gerdau reported Q4 earnings late Wednesday of 0.15 Brazilian reais ($0.03) per share, down from 0.27 reais a year earlier.

Comparable analyst estimates were not readily available.

Net sales for the quarter were 16.82 billion reais, up from 14.72 billion reais a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 16.12 billion reais.

data.title: China, Hong Kong stocks drop on Trump's fresh tariff threats, tech drag
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks dropped on Thursday following U.S. President Donald Trump's fresh tariff threats, though losses were limited as analysts said the rally in Chinese stocks, driven by the DeepSeek breakthrough, still has momentum.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 Index ended the session down 0.3%, while the Shanghai Composite Index was flat, as Beijing left benchmark lending rates unchanged at the monthly fixing.

** Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index declined 1.6%, dragged by a sharp correction in technology stocks.

** Markets in Asia wobbled after Trump said he would announce tariffs related to lumber, cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals "over the next month or sooner."

** Highlighting growing Sino-U.S. frictions, China condemned tariffs launched or threatened by Trump at a World Trade Organization meeting. The U.S. has announced sweeping 10% tariffs on all Chinese imports.

** Markets dropped as traders locked in profits after recent gains, underpinned by Chinese tech shares following the success of start-up DeepSeek, whose low-cost, breakthrough AI model made a global impact.

** But investments banks, including Morgan Stanley, see further upside in China stocks.

** The Wall Street bank upgraded MSCI China and the Hang Seng Index, citing better corporate governance, improved geopolitical conditions, and Beijing's pledge to support the private sector.

** "We believe a structural regime shift is finally happening within China's equity market, especially the offshore space," Morgan Stanley said in a report. "We move from being deeply sceptical to cautiously more optimistic."

** In a sign of performance divergence within China's tech sector, stocks from internet healthcare (.CSI930720), big data (.CSI930745) and telecom services (.CSI932140) continue to power ahead, but cloud computing (.CSI930851) and AI plays (.CSI930713) weakened.

** In Hong Kong, the Hang Seng Tech Index dropped 3% in its worst day in three months, but is still up 20% so far this month.

data.title: Wilsons Upgrades Opthea to Market-Weight from Underweight; Price Target is AU$1
data.description: Opthea has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from AU$0.28 to AU$2.71, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Knorr Bremse climbs on strong Q4 results and positive cash flow
data.description:
** Shares in Knorr Bremse climb 5% after the company posted Q4 order intake and margins surpassed consensus expectations

** The German brake maker's reported FY order intake at 8.19 bln euros ($8.55 billion), and operating EBIT margin at 12.3%

** The company's FY free cash flow at 730 mln euros is above the given guidance back in July between 550-650 mln euros

** J.P. Morgan notes that the free cash flow is also significantly higher than the consensus of 602 mln euros

** "The Q4 results show a resilient performance in the most challenging quarter for the CVS segment since the COVID period and should bode well for its credentials to become a quality name in the sector," says JPM

** It adds that Knorr Bremse's future appears positive as, in their assessment, management do not foresee any supply chain issues with specialist suppliers for the rail industry, which as previously the company's concern

** The stock is at the top of Germany's mid-cap index

($1 = 0.9583 euros)

data.title: Taiwan January export orders down for first time in nearly a year
data.description:
Key points:
  • Export orders -3.0 y/y vs -1.8% Reuters poll forecast
  • Orders from China -18.3x.x% y/y vs +13.6% in prior month
  • Ministry sees February orders between +15.3% and +20.6% y/y

Taiwan's export orders fell for the first time in 11 months in January as demand for technology products softened amid a seasonal slowdown, but the government remained optimistic about ongoing demand for artificial intelligence technologies.

Export orders dropped 3.0% year-on-year in January to $46.97 billion, Taiwan's Ministry of Economic Affairs said on Thursday.

That was worse than a 1.8% contraction forecast in a Reuters poll and compared with December's expansion of 20.8%, though the last week of January coincided with the week-long Lunar New Year holiday in Taiwan.

Orders for goods from Taiwan, home to the world's largest contract chipmaker and other tech companies, are considered a bellwether of global technology demand.

Taiwan's export performance this year could be substantially impacted if U.S. President Donald Trump follows through with his tariff plans.

Trump has floated a proposal for a 25% tariff on chips. Taiwan has responded to the tariff threat with diplomatic overtures and plans to discuss chip investment in the United States.

Still, the ministry was generally upbeat about this year and said it expects export orders in February to rise between 15.3% and 20.6% on year.

"Demand will remain solid for our supply chain of advanced technologies in semiconductors and servers, supporting export orders growth momentum," it said in a statement. However, it added that uncertainties such as geopolitical risks would cloud the global economic growth outlook.

Taiwan's orders in January for telecommunications products were down 13.3% year-on-year, while electronic product orders rose 1.5%.

Overall orders from China fell 18.3% versus a 13.6% gain in December. Orders from the United States were up 6.4%, slowing sharply from a 31% surge the month before.

Orders from Europe fell 12.4% in January, while orders from Japan were up 8.0%.

data.title: Hong Kong Inflation Rate Rises to 4-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Hong Kong rose to 2% in January 2025, after holding steady at 1.4% for three consecutive months.

This marked the highest inflation rate since September 2024, as prices increased at a faster pace for food (1.2% vs 1% in December), housing (1.5% vs 1%), electricity, gas and water (13.2% vs 11.4%), transport (4% vs 2.1%), and miscellaneous services (2.8% vs 2.1%).

In addition, deflation eased for durable goods (-1% vs -1.6%).

Meanwhile, costs moderated for alcoholic beverages and tobacco (20.7% vs 21.2%) and miscellaneous goods (0.8% vs 1.4%).

Moreover, deflation worsened for clothing and footwear (-3.4% vs -3.2%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices edged higher to 0.4% in January 2025, the most since June 2024, from 0.1% in the previous month.

data.title: Juventus tumbles after Champions League knockout
data.description:
** Shares in Italy's top-tier football club Juventus fall as much as 11.4% following the Champions League elimination in the knockout phase playoff against PSV Eindhoven

** A Milan-based trader says that the Turin-based club lost TV rights income connected with the European tournament

** The trader points out that after Tether minority stake acquisition the stock rallied up around 24% in the last trading sessions and this is the right chance to realize gains

** Failing to qualify for the Champions League round of 16 also calls the club strategy into question, even if it is a speculative dynamic, the trader adds

** Juventus stands in fourth place in Italy's Serie A table with 46 points

** The club became the third Italian side eliminated from the competition following AC Milan and Atalanta on Tuesday

** Including Thursday's fall the stock has dropped by 14.5% YTD

data.title: Italian Stocks Slightly Higher
data.description:
The FTSE MIB edged higher to around 38,408 in early trading on Thursday, slightly recovering from the previous session’s losses, as traders digested the latest FOMC minutes and monitored a fresh round of corporate earnings.

Meanwhile, attention remained on global trade policies and ongoing Ukraine war peace talks.

Among individual stocks, top performers included Davide Campari-Milano (+2.9%), STMicroelectronics (+1.8%), Bper Banca (+1.6%), and Banco BPM SPA (+1%).

On the downside, Tenaris was the index’s worst performer, dropping more than 4%.

data.title: EU Clears EUR920 Million German Aid for Infineon Chip Facility
data.description:
The European Commission approved 920 million euros in German state aid for Infineon Technologies' planned semiconductor manufacturing facility on Thursday.

The German semiconductor company's new Megafab-DD project in Dresden, Germany, has a total investment tag of 3.5 billion euros. It will be the first of its kind in Europe and is expected to reach full capacity in 2031.

Infineon will receive the aid in the form of a direct grant.

data.title: UK's Indivior shares tumble on lower 2025 revenue forecast
data.description:
British drugmaker Indivior on Thursday projected a 17% fall in 2025 net revenue at the midpoint of its forecast range, partly hurt by weak sales of its film-form medication-assisted therapy for opiate addiction, sending its shares sharply lower.

Shares in the company fell as much as 22.8% to a near four-month low of 670.5 pence in early trade.

CEO Mark Crossley said the steep fall expected in 2025 net revenue was primarily due to a more than 50% drop at its opiate addiction therapy, Suboxone film, amid intense generic pricing pressures and the potential entry of a fifth generic competitor.

In October last year, Indivior issued its second profit and revenue warning in three months citing weak sales of its opioid addiction treatment Sublocade due to competition from Swedish pharmaceutical firm Camurus' drug Brixadi.

Indivior, which has a market value of just over 1 billion pounds ($1.26 billion), reported a 16% rise in adjusted operating profit to $312 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

After a 9% increase in net revenue to $1.19 billion last year, the company expects between $955 million and $1.03 billion for 2025.

($1 = 0.7935 pounds)

data.title: BE Semiconductor Shares Drop After Demand Weakness Weighs on Results
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

BE Semiconductor Industries shares fell after the company reported fourth-quarter revenue that was below analysts' expectations, hit by lower demand for automotive applications, and projected another decline this quarter.

Shares in the Dutch supplier of equipment to the semiconductor industry were down 5.9% at 109.40 euros European morning trade Thursday, having dropped 10% earlier, and fell about 29% over the past year.

The company, also known as Besi, said revenue for the fourth quarter was 153.4 million euros ($159.9 million), down 2% compared with the year-earlier period. Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected revenue of 160.9 million euros.

Net profit jumped 27% to 59.3 million euros, boosted by a tax benefit. Gross margin declined by 0.7 percentage points to 64%.

"Besi's fourth quarter results were adversely affected by ongoing weakness in mainstream assembly markets, seasonal influences and lower demand for hybrid bonding and photonics applications as customers digested capacity added in 2024," Chief Executive and President Richard W. Blickman said.

A mainstream assembly recovery is likely to begin in the second half of 2025, Blickman said.

For the first quarter, Besi projects a decline of up to 10% in revenue relative to the fourth quarter and a gross margin of 63% to 65%.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: DAX on Cautious Note
data.description:
The DAX traded flat to slightly higher around 22,470 on Thursday, following sharp losses the previous day, as traders monitored a fresh round of corporate earnings from European companies.

Meanwhile, the focus remained on global trade policies and the evolving situation in Ukraine.

On the corporate front, Mercedes-Benz Group AG reported weak profit and revenues in its fourth quarter, and also gave a bleak outlook for 2025 after a 30% drop in 2024's earnings due to weakness in key markets China and Europe.

** Stock among top losers on FTSE mid-cap index (.FTMC) which is up 0.14%

** British oilfield services and engineering firm's CFO steps down after incorrect description of professional qualifications

** Stock tumbled 62% in 2024

data.title: US real estate firm CoStar prepares $1.7 billion bid for Australia's Domain, source says
data.description:
U.S. real estate firm CoStar is preparing an A$2.7 billion ($1.72 billion) bid for Australian property classifieds firm Domain Holdings , said a person with direct knowledge of the matter.

CoStar declined to comment and Domain did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters.

CoStar began buying Domain stock at A$4.20 a share on Thursday and has gained control of as much as 19% of the company, the person said.

The person declined to be identified discussing confidential information.

A term sheet seen by Reuters, which did not identify the buyer of the stock, said the suitor intended to launch a full takeover at A$4.20 a share.

The offer is a 34.6% premium to Domain's closing share price on Thursday of $A3.12.

($1 = 1.5689 Australian dollars)
